name: library
description: Hierarchical documentation library — the org's Confluence replacement. v1.3.0 (Release C.1.4.0 — libownerxfer): catalog pointers are keyed on the doc's stable id (not owner_hash), so ownership/visibility/rename changes rewrite the pointer IN PLACE — never a rename that strands an undeletable duplicate on the Shared-Drive index; a new transfer-doc-ownership capability performs the Drive ownership change AND the catalog/meta rewrite atomically, hard-gated on an independent ACL check (gdrive-only positive path; NOT_IMPLEMENTED on OneDrive/SharePoint); find-doc and review-report now collapse/flag duplicate pointers by doc id and flag legacy owner_hash-keyed filenames for admin migration. v1.2.0 (Release C.1.3.7 — catalog/ACL reconcile): find-doc (1.2.0) and review-report (1.1.0) now reconcile a pointer's owner/scope against the actual ACL — when a doc's ownership or sharing changed outside the library (raw transferOwnership, out-of-band unshare, owner_departed) the pointer can go stale, so find-doc verifies via aifs_get_permissions on read and trusts the ACL (healing the pointer if writable, else flagging), and review-report sweeps for pointer/ACL drift. Closes bug 20260706-8d20ea22-libptrstale. v1.1.0 (Release C.1.3 — crossdriveread): private/shared docs now carry an `item_drive_id` in their pointer (create-doc + set-doc-visibility capture it from aifs_stat's `drive_id`), and find-doc opens shared private docs via the cross-drive anchor `id:{item_drive_id}:{folder_id}/doc.md` — fixing 'discoverable but unopenable' for a doc held in the owner's personal OneDrive (a bare anchor resolved against the reader's own drive and returned PATH_NOT_FOUND). Requires onedrive adapter 2.3.0+; harmless on gdrive (native shared-content reads). Named groups of documents with unlimited-depth sub-groups form an always-org-public taxonomy; document TITLES and tree placement are visible to every member, while document CONTENT is org-public by default or can be set private (owner + explicit grant list, content held in the owner's My Drive) at any time including creation. Discovery runs off an always-public catalog (/shared/library-index/: tree.json, per-doc pointers, doc-types.json) decoupled from content ACLs, so private docs still appear as titles. Rich retrieval metadata (type, tags, summary) is indexed for PUBLIC docs only and is stripped when a doc goes private. v1.0.0: groups, docs-with-images, public/private visibility with member-applied grants, governed doc-type vocabulary (admin locked/open), staleness/review tracking (180-day default), comms-channel access requests. Reuses the projects 4.0 two-tier + pointer-index patterns. Requires agent-index-core 3.9.0+, adapter 2.5.0+, permission-change-helper 0.4.1+.
author: agent-index

Create a new document in the library

triggers.description: Create and file a new document

triggers.description: Adjust a document's visibility or grants

triggers.description: Send an access request to the doc owner

external_dependencies.access_required: Read/write to /shared/library/ and /shared/library-index/, and to the owner's My Drive for private docs, via aifs_* tools
Member-applied additive grants on private document folders (binary 0.4.1+)
Deliver access-request notifications via the org's configured comms channel
